source_note: ICRC internal — Claude API + web search. Field validation required. Sources: Kyiv Independent, Ukrainska Pravda, Reuters/USNews, RBC-Ukraine, OSW, ISW/CriticalThreats, TheDefensePost/AFP, GlobalBanking&Finance, news-pravda.com, PBS, Wikipedia (2025 Sumy Offensive), DRC, HALO Trust. Cross-referenced minimum 2 sources per incident where available.

incidents.description: Rare daytime drone attack on Kyiv; 211 drones launched, 194 downed. Debris fell near Independence Monument (Maidan) and in Solomyanskyi, Svyatoshynskyi districts.

incidents.cible: Urban public areas / ICRC HQ district
incidents.description: Drone debris from 16/03 attack fell near Independence Monument and open areas in Solomyanskyi and Svyatoshynskyi districts. Dry grass fire ignited in Svyatoshynskyi.
Russian EW/GPS jamming reported across Kyiv area coinciding with March 16 drone attack. Air force confirmed drones used advanced mesh-network communication channels disrupting AD coordination.
Artillery and mortar shelling continued in Seredyna-Buda hromada (border raion). Civilian injuries reported; pattern consistent with daily attacks throughout March 2026.

Russian drone struck Trostyanets-Smorodino locomotive station; technical inspection point and diagnostic equipment damaged; locomotive preparation disrupted. Railway access implications for humanitarian logistics.
incidents.source: news-pravda.com / OSW Centre for Eastern Studies — 18/03/2026

incidents.description: Two civilians killed in Krasnopillia hromada due to Russian guided aerial bomb strikes on 20 March. Russian forces also used FPV drones (8 explosions), artillery (10), mortars (42) in same hromada.
Russian forces continued offensive operations in Sumy Oblast on 20 March; attacks recorded NW of Sumy (Sopych), N of Sumy (Kindrativka, Novomykolaivka), NE of Sumy City. No advance confirmed.
Russian Geran/Shahed drone strike on Chernihiv energy infrastructure; Nizhynska substation hit. Entire city of Chernihiv lost power; 400,000+ subscribers affected incl. Slavutych (Kyiv Oblast).

incidents.cible: Regional power grid / civilian energy supply
incidents.description: Post-strike: Chernihiv city administration confirmed complete blackout. Chernihivoblenergo stated energy facility damaged; repair work delayed pending security clearance. 10 train routes cancelled/rerouted.
Drone strike on Trostyanets-Smorodino locomotive depot; Maintenance Shop No.2 and equipment destroyed; fire reported; railway track damaged. Disrupts humanitarian supply route.
incidents.source: news-pravda.com / OSW Centre for Eastern Studies — 21/03/2026

incidents.description: Russian airstrike on central settlement of Krasnopillia community on 21 March; large fire; administrative buildings and homes destroyed. 1 body recovered from rubble, 2 injured. 12 communities struck in total.

incidents.cible: Civilian energy supply (spillover from Chernihiv attack)
incidents.description: Slavutych (Kyiv Oblast, Vyshhorod raion) lost power due to Chernihiv energy grid attack. Affects ~400,000 combined subscribers. Emergency repairs activated.
Continued strikes on 12 border communities (incl. Seredyna-Buda, Krasnopillia, Yunakivka, Shalyhyne) severely restricted humanitarian access. Evacuations ongoing under fire risk.

Russian MoD claimed capture of border village Potapivka (Sumy direction) on 22 March. Russian northern group expanding buffer zone. Ukraine did not immediately confirm. Civilian displacement reported.
State Emergency Service evacuated 31 civilians incl. 3 children from Sumy Oblast border area on 22 March. Access constrained by active hostilities; corridors intermittently impassable.
incidents.source: Wikipedia/2025 Sumy Offensive citing Ukrainian media — 22/03/2026

incidents.description: Russian drone attack overnight on Brovary (Kyiv Oblast); 3 commercial enterprises damaged, 2 homes and several vehicles struck; fire contained. No casualties officially reported by Kyiv Oblast Governor.

Russian forces advanced up to 500m in Krasnopillia district on 22 March; assault units broke through near Novodmytrivka forest area. Increased risk of crossfire for civilians and humanitarian convoys.
Consecutive air raid alerts from ~08:26 to 11:44 on 16 March disrupted daily movement in Kyiv City. Daytime curfew-equivalent conditions; ICRC staff movement constrained during alert periods.

Ongoing mortar and artillery fire recorded in Shalyhyne hromada (border area). Consistent with daily attack patterns. Civilian population under evacuation orders; humanitarian access severely restricted.

Chernihiv Oblast confirmed among most mine/ERW-contaminated regions in Ukraine (UN 2024 assessment). Ongoing humanitarian demining operations by HALO Trust across Chernihiv. ERW risk remains elevated.
incidents.source: DRC / HALO Trust / Monitor — background/persistent risk confirmed 2026

incidents.description: Residual landmine/UXO contamination in Bucha-Irpin area (Kyiv Oblast) confirmed as persistent hazard. HALO Trust conducting clearance; risk to returning civilians and humanitarian convoys on secondary roads.
incidents.source: HALO Trust / DRC — persistent risk, Kyiv Oblast 2022-2026

incidents.cible: Humanitarian mobility / road and rail access
incidents.description: Complete Chernihiv power blackout following 21 March drone strike disrupted rail links; 10 passenger/freight trains cancelled or rerouted. Road access unaffected but blackout complicates field operations.

Russian disinformation narratives targeting Ukrainian mobilisation/morale noted as deliberate campaign per USCC research. Potential bleed-over to perception of neutral humanitarian actors, including ICRC.
incidents.source: Foreign Policy / Wikipedia conscription crisis — context March 2026

incidents.description: Persistent aerial threat rhythm (daily alerts, multiple daytime attacks in March 2026) creates chronic stress environment for ICRC Kyiv HQ staff. Operational fatigue risk elevated following consecutive March escalations.

Growing TCC-civilian confrontations in Kyiv during mobilisation raids. 341 recorded assaults on recruiters in 2025; 100+ in early 2026. Social tensions risk spill-over affecting humanitarian movement.
incidents.source: Washington Spokesman-Review / Fairplanet — April 2026 context, ongoing since early 2026

sra_updates.rationale: Three confirmed incidents (16/03): rare daytime multi-wave Shahed/Lancet attack targeting central Kyiv including ICRC HQ district; debris fell near Independence Monument; 211 drones launched. Pattern shift to daytime operations increases ICRC staff exposure during business hours.

Three incidents confirmed within the reporting period (20–21 March): 2 KIA + 2 WIA in Krasnopillia on 20/03 from guided aerial bombs; 1 KIA + 2 WIA in Krasnopillia on 21/03 from airstrike; 12 communities struck on 21/03 incl. Seredyna-Buda, Shalyhyne, Yunakivka — all ICRC-operational hromadas. Aerial bomb campaign intensifying.

Two converging incidents on 21/03: Nizhynska substation (330 kV) hit by Geran drones; entire Chernihiv city lost power (400,000+ subscribers); 10 train routes suspended. Repair work delayed by security constraints. Third supporting context: Chernihiv repeated as priority energy target across winter 2025–spring 2026. Operational ICRC impact on field teams and local partners confirmed via rail disruption.

Four incidents during period confirm near-total denial of humanitarian access to Seredyna-Buda, Shalyhyne, Krasnopillia, Yunakivka: active combat in border hromadas, Russian advance into Potapivka on 22/03, only 31 civilians evacuated under fire risk, railway node at Trostyanets damaged twice. ICRC field access to priority population groups in border raions effectively suspended.
